summary.rationale: The songs workspace keeps its existing editorial list-detail structure while the repeated warm neutrals, metadata roles, and headline rhythm now map more clearly to the draft token baseline.

findings.message: The selected song card still uses local inverse colors instead of semantic tokens, which is acceptable for contrast today but leaves one visible styling path outside the shared token vocabulary.
findings.recommendation: Introduce semantic inverse tokens in a later pass if more surfaces need this treatment.

notes: The workspace layout and selection flow are unchanged.
Repeated color and type roles now rely more on shared token bindings.
Lead-sheet readability and list scanability are preserved.
Selected-state contrast remains strong because the darker treatment was not over-normalized.
The warm paper-and-stone palette remains intact.
Typography rhythm now maps more directly to the draft display and metadata tracking rules.
The surface remains warm, restrained, and editorial rather than drifting back toward default cool utility styling.

next_steps: Consider semantic inverse tokens for selected cards if token alignment expands to other list surfaces.
Normalize remaining local spacing literals in the songs surface only if a later pass shows real duplication pressure.
